rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARION B BECKLAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The conservator does not object to the Court's withdrawal of the conservator's authority to take fees on account as set out in the 09/11/2014 order. However, the conservator requests blanket authority to transfer funds to a set-aside account without petitioning ex parte for court authorization prior to each transfer. The petition caption and notice of the hearing did not refer to this request. The prayers for relief set forth in the petition and proposed order did not include the request, either. 2) The petition does not request compensation of the conservator and her attorneys for their services rendered during this account period. However, the conservator and her attorneys wish to reserve their right to seek such compensation when funds become available or upon termination of this conservatorship. No conditions have been proposed for payment and collection of unpaid fees ($13,851) of the conservator and her attorneys. 3) The court-appointed attorney filed a declaration requesting termination of the conservator's authority under Probate Code sections 2355, 2356.5(b), and 2356.5(c). The conservator supports the request to terminate her authority under section 2356.5 (b) & (c), but desires to retain her authority under section 2355. The petition caption and notice of the hearing did not refer to this request. The prayers for relief set forth in the petition included termination of the Public Guardian's section 2356.5(b) & (c) authority, but the proposed order did not. No petition has been filed under section 2359 (applied by section 2356.5(h)). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KYRA MARY BAELE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution; For Allowance On Account Of Compensation To Administrator And To Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. The Court now requires an appearance for a petition for preliminary distribution. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. Other issues that might need to be addressed: All of the cash on hand ($1,070,221.33) is reportedly held in one checking account, therefore, not fully insured. No bank statement has been filed to verify the balance of cash reported to be on hand. Is the administrator's allegation of the amount sufficient since there was no accounting? No accounting has been presented yet and no waivers of accounting have been filed, although the decedent died over three years ago. [LRSF 14.34A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FUMIKO TANAKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/04/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Examiner has not received the required courtesy copy of the amended petition. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES RUTH HIGGINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/10/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amendment to the petition (NOT an "amended petition" - see CRC 7.3), while uploaded and file-stamped 3/28/22, was not actually e-filed until 3/29/22, when the pleading was available for viewing. Furthermore, notice of the as-yet-unfiled pleading was reportedly given on 3/25/22, making notice defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The face of the notice says that the administrator "has filed" and that the person receiving the notice may "refer to the filed documents," which statements are not true until the pleading is e-filed. The petition, as amended, is still missing the disclosures required to be made pursuant to Probate Code Sections 1064(a)(4) and (5). A personal representative has a duty to retain cash in an interest-bearing account. Why is cash on hand exactly the same as shown on the I&A? [Probate Code Section 9652(a).] Probate Code Section 9202(b) requires notice to the Director of the California Victim Compensation Board, if applicable, not to the DHCS. The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450.00. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/27/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ZHANNA V. RUSSELL LIVING TRUST DATED 7/27/20 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Confirming Assets To Revocable Trust With Consent Of All Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/22. A supplement to a pleading does not require additional notice of hearing, but a copy of a supplement to a pleading must be served if service of a copy of the pleading was required, unless waived by the court. [CRC 7.53(b).] Proof of service of a copy of the supplement filed on 3/30/22 must be filed, showing that the copy of the supplement was served at least 30 days before 5/18/22. (Issues raised by an Examiner should be addressed in pleadings that stand on their own and not refer to Examiner's Notes, which are, unless attached to the pleading by the petitioner herself, not filed for the record.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BERNARD PASCAL SUTTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/3/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain what box petition was trying to check on page 1 under #2. (d) petitioner checked in between boxes 2 and 3. If petitioner was trying to check box 2 then the amount of bond must be specified and must be rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars. [LRSF 14.16 L.] If petitioner was trying to check box 3 then on page 2 under 3.e. a box must be selected. 2) Laurent Lecellier's name, relation to decedent, age, and address must be listed on page 4 under #8. 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID JUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/31/22. (No earlier availability.) No courtesy copies. No proposed order. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately, conventionally, and confidentially. Courtesy copies and a proposed order were not received by the assigned Examiner by 4/4/22, as posted in the tentative ruling on 3/30/22, or even as of 4/6/22. Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/19/22 to avoid another automatic continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
